fixes_applied.description: Moved module-level docstring from inside function definition to module level (before imports). The docstring now correctly appears at the top of the file describing the test case.

Created GeminiRAHelper.pause_subscription() helper method in gemini_ra_helper_additions.py to encapsulate Rails Admin pause operation. Replaced inline page.locator() calls in precondition 4 with single helper method call: GeminiRAHelper.pause_subscription(page, tenant_email)
This violation was resolved when V001 was fixed - both violations referred to the same issue of docstring placement

Removed unused GlobalState import from 'from core.settings import framework_logger, GlobalState'. Changed to 'from core.settings import framework_logger'. Updated device_type references to use common.GlobalState.device_type with conditional check.

Consolidated verbose logging in precondition setup. Removed 3 separate log statements and replaced with single concise log: 'Preconditions 1-3 completed: Enrollment with 50 pages plan, verified subscribed status without free months'
Consolidated verbose logging in step 3. Removed 2 consecutive log statements and replaced with single concise log: 'Step 3: Time shifted 32 days and billing triggered successfully'

Removed standalone 'class Elements:' wrapper from page additions file. Replaced with clear comment indicating attributes should be added to EXISTING Elements class. This prevents confusion during manual merge and ensures correct integration pattern.

fix_actions_applied: MULTI_FILE_FIX × 1 (helper method created and inline code replaced)

unfixed_violations.reason: This was identified as a false positive in the scoring report. The expect() call already has an explicit timeout=30000. No fix needed.
This was identified as a false positive in the scoring report. The test script correctly uses the page object pattern with print_history_page.{element_name} accessing properties. No fix needed.

new_files_created.reason: Created to encapsulate inline Rails Admin pause subscription interactions as prescribed by violation V002 (MULTI_FILE_FIX)

post_fix_summary.expected_score_improvement: Expected new score: ~96/100 (7 violations fixed: 2 CRITICAL × 3 = 6 points, 3 MODERATE × 2 = 6 points, 2 MINOR × 1 = 2 points = 14 points recovered, but 2 false positives remain = 4 point deduction)
post_fix_summary.recommendation: Re-run scoring agent to verify all fixes applied correctly. The two remaining violations (V005 and V006) are false positives that should be corrected in the scoring logic.
